#!/bin/bash
# Compliance Validation - Ensure mandatory services are running

LOG="/var/log/aztau-soe-compliance.log"
log() { echo "[SOE_COMPLIANCE] $(date '+%H:%M:%S') [$1] $2" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

SENTINEL="/var/lib/soe-compliance/allow-run"
if [ ! -f "$SENTINEL" ]; then
    log "INFO" "Delegate to user-script to run the compliance check at its first boot."
    exit 0
fi

log "INFO" "Starting compliance validation"

# Configuration with defaults
RETRY=${retry_attempts:-3}
DELAY=${retry_delay:-5}
TIMEOUT=${start_timeout:-30}

# Service definitions
SERVICES=(
"falcon-sensor"
"qualys-cloud-agent"
"oneagent"
"rsyslog"
)

# Helper functions
exists() { systemctl list-unit-files --type=service 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^$1.service"; }
status() { systemctl is-active "$1" 2>/dev/null || echo "inactive"; }
start() {
    local svc="$1"
    log "INFO" "Starting $svc"
    for i in $(seq 1 $RETRY); do
        timeout $TIMEOUT systemctl start "$svc" && sleep 2 && [ "$(status "$svc")" = "active" ] && {
            log "SUCCESS" "$svc started"
            systemctl enable "$svc" 2>/dev/null || true
            return 0
        }
        log "WARN" "Attempt $i/$RETRY failed for $svc"
        [ $i -lt $RETRY ] && sleep $DELAY
    done
    log "ERROR" "Failed to start $svc"
    return 1
}

# Main validation
failed=() success=0

[ ${#SERVICES[@]} -eq 0 ] && {
    log "INFO" "No services configured, skipping"
    echo "SOE_COMPLIANCE_STATUS=SKIPPED" > /var/log/aztau-soe-compliance-status
    exit 0
}

# Get instance ID
token=$(curl -s --max-time 5 -X PUT "http://169.254.169.254/latest/api/token" -H "X-aws-ec2-metadata-token-ttl-seconds: 21600" 2>/dev/null)
instance=$(curl -s --max-time 5 -H "X-aws-ec2-metadata-token: $token" http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/instance-id 2>/dev/null)
[ -n "$instance" ] && log "INFO" "Instance: $instance"

# Process services
for svc in "${SERVICES[@]}"; do
    log "INFO" "Validating $svc"
    if ! exists "$svc"; then
        log "ERROR" "$svc not found - validation failed"
        failed+=("$svc")
    else
        st=$(status "$svc")
        log "INFO" "$svc status: $st"
        if [ "$st" = "active" ]; then
            log "SUCCESS" "$svc validated"
            ((success++))
        elif start "$svc"; then
            log "SUCCESS" "$svc started and validated"
            ((success++))
        else
            log "ERROR" "$svc failed to start"
            failed+=("$svc")
        fi
    fi
done

# Summary and final decision
log "INFO" "Complete - Success: $success, Failed: ${#failed[@]}"

if [ ${#failed[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    echo "SOE_COMPLIANCE_STATUS=FAILED" > /var/log/aztau-soe-compliance-status
    log "ERROR" "Services failed: ${failed[*]}"
    log "FATAL" "Compliance validation failed - halting system to prevent non-compliant instance"

    # Graceful shutdown with poweroff - ensures AWS shows instance as "stopped"
    shutdown -h now "Compliance validation failed - security services not running"

    # Backup in case halt is delayed
    sleep 5
    exit 1
else
    echo "SOE_COMPLIANCE_STATUS=PASSED" > /var/log/aztau-soe-compliance-status
    log "SUCCESS" "All $success services validated successfully - instance is compliant"
fi

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

SENTINEL_DIR="/var/lib/soe-compliance"
SENTINEL_FILE="$SENTINEL_DIR/allow-run"
TARGET="/var/lib/cloud/scripts/per-boot/part-002-compliance-perboot.sh"

log() { echo "[FINAL] $*" >&2; }

setup_sentinel() {
  install -d -m 700 "$SENTINEL_DIR"
  echo "authorized" > "$SENTINEL_FILE"
  chmod 600 "$SENTINEL_FILE"
  log "Sentinel established at $SENTINEL_FILE"
}

invoke_compliance() {
  if [ -x "$TARGET" ]; then
    log "Invoking per-boot compliance script: $TARGET"
    if ! "$TARGET"; then
      rc=$?
      log "$TARGET returned $rc"
      return $rc
    fi
  else
    log "Compliance script not found at $TARGET"
    return 127
  fi
}

main() {
  setup_sentinel
  invoke_compliance
}

main "$@"

create.description: Whether to create the Cloudwatch log group

kms_key_id.description: The ARN of the KMS Key to use when encrypting logs

name.description: A name for the log group

retention_in_days.description: Specifies the number of days you want to retain log events in the specified log group. Possible values are: 0, 1, 3, 5, 7, 14, 30, 60, 90, 120, 150, 180, 365, 400, 545, 731, 1096, 1827, 2192, 2557, 2922, 3288 and 3653.

tags.description: A map of tags to add to Cloudwatch log group

autoscaling_group_arn.description: The ARN for this AutoScaling Group

autoscaling_group_availability_zones.description: The availability zones of the autoscale group

autoscaling_group_default_cooldown.description: Time between a scaling activity and the succeeding scaling activity

autoscaling_group_desired_capacity.description: The number of Amazon EC2 instances that should be running in the group

autoscaling_group_enabled_metrics.description: List of metrics enabled for collection

autoscaling_group_health_check_grace_per.description: Time after instance comes into service before checking health

autoscaling_group_health_check_type.description: EC2 or ELB. Controls how health checking is done

autoscaling_group_load_balancers.description: The load balancer names associated with the autoscaling group

autoscaling_group_max_size.description: The maximum size of the autoscale group

autoscaling_group_min_size.description: The minimum size of the autoscale group

autoscaling_group_target_group_arns.description: List of Target Group ARNs that apply to this AutoScaling Group

autoscaling_group_tags.description: A map of additional tags to add to the autoscaling group

availability_zone_distribution.description: A map of configuration for capacity distribution across availability zones

availability_zones.description: A list of one or more availability zones for the group. Used for EC2-Classic and default subnets when not specified with `vpc_zone_identifier` argument. Conflicts with `vpc_zone_identifier`

capacity_rebalance.description: Indicates whether capacity rebalance is enabled

create.description: Determines whether to create autoscaling group or not

create_scaling_policy.description: Determines whether to create target scaling policy schedule or not

default_cooldown.description: The amount of time, in seconds, after a scaling activity completes before another scaling activity can start

default_instance_warmup.description: Amount of time, in seconds, until a newly launched instance can contribute to the Amazon CloudWatch metrics. This delay lets an instance finish initializing before Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling aggregates instance metrics, resulting in more reliable usage data. Set this value equal to the amount of time that it takes for resource consumption to become stable after an instance reaches the InService state.

delete_timeout.description: Delete timeout to wait for destroying autoscaling group

desired_capacity.description: The number of Amazon EC2 instances that should be running in the autoscaling group

desired_capacity_type.description: The unit of measurement for the value specified for desired_capacity. Supported for attribute-based instance type selection only. Valid values: `units`, `vcpu`, `memory-mib`.

enabled_metrics.description: A list of metrics to collect. The allowed values are `GroupDesiredCapacity`, `GroupInServiceCapacity`, `GroupPendingCapacity`, `GroupMinSize`, `GroupMaxSize`, `GroupInServiceInstances`, `GroupPendingInstances`, `GroupStandbyInstances`, `GroupStandbyCapacity`, `GroupTerminatingCapacity`, `GroupTerminatingInstances`, `GroupTotalCapacity`, `GroupTotalInstances`

force_delete.description: Allows deleting the Auto Scaling Group without waiting for all instances in the pool to terminate. You can force an Auto Scaling Group to delete even if it's in the process of scaling a resource. Normally, Terraform drains all the instances before deleting the group. This bypasses that behavior and potentially leaves resources dangling

health_check_grace_period.description: Time (in seconds) after instance comes into service before checking health

health_check_type.description: `EC2` or `ELB`. Controls how health checking is done

ignore_failed_scaling_activities.description: Whether to ignore failed Auto Scaling scaling activities while waiting for capacity. The default is false -- failed scaling activities cause errors to be returned.

initial_lifecycle_hooks.description: One or more Lifecycle Hooks to attach to the Auto Scaling Group before instances are launched. The syntax is exactly the same as the separate `aws_autoscaling_lifecycle_hook` resource, without the `autoscaling_group_name` attribute. Please note that this will only work when creating a new Auto Scaling Group. For all other use-cases, please use `aws_autoscaling_lifecycle_hook` resource

instance_maintenance_policy.description: If this block is configured, add a instance maintenance policy to the specified Auto Scaling group

instance_name.description: Name that is propogated to launched EC2 instances via a tag - if not provided, defaults to `var.name`

instance_refresh.description: If this block is configured, start an Instance Refresh when this Auto Scaling Group is updated

launch_template_id.description: ID of an existing launch template to be used (created outside of this module)

launch_template_version.description: Launch template version. Can be version number, `$Latest`, or `$Default`

max_instance_lifetime.description: The maximum amount of time, in seconds, that an instance can be in service, values must be either equal to 0 or between 86400 and 31536000 seconds

max_size.description: The maximum size of the autoscaling group

metrics_granularity.description: The granularity to associate with the metrics to collect. The only valid value is `1Minute`

min_elb_capacity.description: Setting this causes Terraform to wait for this number of instances to show up healthy in the ELB only on creation. Updates will not wait on ELB instance number changes

min_size.description: The minimum size of the autoscaling group

name.description: Name used across the resources created

placement_group.description: The name of the placement group into which you'll launch your instances, if any

protect_from_scale_in.description: Allows setting instance protection. The autoscaling group will not select instances with this setting for termination during scale in events.

scaling_policies.description: Map of target scaling policy schedule to create

service_linked_role_arn.description: The ARN of the service-linked role that the ASG will use to call other AWS services

suspended_processes.description: A list of processes to suspend for the Auto Scaling Group. The allowed values are `Launch`, `Terminate`, `HealthCheck`, `ReplaceUnhealthy`, `AZRebalance`, `AlarmNotification`, `ScheduledActions`, `AddToLoadBalancer`, `InstanceRefresh`. Note that if you suspend either the `Launch` or `Terminate` process types, it can prevent your Auto Scaling Group from functioning properly

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to resources

termination_policies.description: A list of policies to decide how the instances in the Auto Scaling Group should be terminated. The allowed values are `OldestInstance`, `NewestInstance`, `OldestLaunchConfiguration`, `ClosestToNextInstanceHour`, `OldestLaunchTemplate`, `AllocationStrategy`, `Default`

traffic_source_attachments.description: Map of traffic source attachment definitions to create

use_mixed_instances_policy.description: Determines whether to use a mixed instances policy in the autoscaling group or not

use_name_prefix.description: Determines whether to use `name` as is or create a unique name beginning with the `name` as the prefix

vpc_zone_identifier.description: A list of subnet IDs to launch resources in. Subnets automatically determine which availability zones the group will reside. Conflicts with `availability_zones`

wait_for_capacity_timeout.description: A maximum duration that Terraform should wait for ASG instances to be healthy before timing out. (See also Waiting for Capacity below.) Setting this to '0' causes Terraform to skip all Capacity Waiting behavior.

wait_for_elb_capacity.description: Setting this will cause Terraform to wait for exactly this number of healthy instances in all attached load balancers on both create and update operations. Takes precedence over `min_elb_capacity` behavior.

arn.description: The ID and ARN of the load balancer we created

arn_suffix.description: ARN suffix of our load balancer - can be used with CloudWatch

dns_name.description: The DNS name of the load balancer

id.description: The ID and ARN of the load balancer we created

listener_rules.description: Map of listeners rules created and their attributes

listeners.description: Map of listeners created and their attributes

target_groups.description: Map of target groups created and their attributes

zone_id.description: The zone_id of the load balancer to assist with creating DNS records

access_logs.description: Map containing access logging configuration for load balancer

client_keep_alive.description: Client keep alive value in seconds. The valid range is 60-604800 seconds. The default is 3600 seconds.

connection_logs.description: Map containing connection logging configuration for load balancer

create.description: Controls if resources should be created (affects nearly all resources)

customer_owned_ipv4_pool.description: The ID of the customer owned ipv4 pool to use for this load balancer

default_port.description: Default port used across the listener and target group

default_protocol.description: Default protocol used across the listener and target group

desync_mitigation_mode.description: Determines how the load balancer handles requests that might pose a security risk to an application due to HTTP desync. Valid values are `monitor`, `defensive` (default), `strictest`

dns_record_client_routing_policy.description: Indicates how traffic is distributed among the load balancer Availability Zones. Possible values are any_availability_zone (default), availability_zone_affinity, or partial_availability_zone_affinity. Only valid for network type load balancers.

drop_invalid_header_fields.description: Indicates whether HTTP headers with header fields that are not valid are removed by the load balancer (`true`) or routed to targets (`false`). The default is `true`. Elastic Load Balancing requires that message header names contain only alphanumeric characters and hyphens. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`

enable_cross_zone_load_balancing.description: If `true`, cross-zone load balancing of the load balancer will be enabled. For application load balancer this feature is always enabled (`true`) and cannot be disabled. Defaults to `true`

enable_deletion_protection.description: If `true`, deletion of the load balancer will be disabled via the AWS API. This will prevent Terraform from deleting the load balancer. Defaults to `true`

enable_http2.description: Indicates whether HTTP/2 is enabled in application load balancers. Defaults to `true`

enable_tls_version_and_cipher_suite_head.description: Indicates whether the two headers (`x-amzn-tls-version` and `x-amzn-tls-cipher-suite`), which contain information about the negotiated TLS version and cipher suite, are added to the client request before sending it to the target. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`. Defaults to `false`

enable_waf_fail_open.description: Indicates whether to allow a WAF-enabled load balancer to route requests to targets if it is unable to forward the request to AWS WAF. Defaults to `false`

enable_xff_client_port.description: Indicates whether the X-Forwarded-For header should preserve the source port that the client used to connect to the load balancer in `application` load balancers. Defaults to `false`

enforce_security_group_inbound_rules_on_.description: Indicates whether inbound security group rules are enforced for traffic originating from a PrivateLink. Only valid for Load Balancers of type network. The possible values are on and off.

idle_timeout.description: The time in seconds that the connection is allowed to be idle. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`. Default: `60`

internal.description: If true, the LB will be internal. Defaults to `false`

ip_address_type.description: The type of IP addresses used by the subnets for your load balancer. The possible values are `ipv4` and `dualstack`

ipam_pools.description: The IPAM pools to use with the load balancer

listeners.description: Map of listener configurations to create

load_balancer_type.description: The type of load balancer to create. Possible values are `application`, `gateway`, or `network`. The default value is `application`

name.description: The name of the LB. This name must be unique within your AWS account, can have a maximum of 32 characters, must contain only alphanumeric characters or hyphens, and must not begin or end with a hyphen

name_prefix.description: Creates a unique name beginning with the specified prefix. Conflicts with `name`

preserve_host_header.description: Indicates whether the Application Load Balancer should preserve the Host header in the HTTP request and send it to the target without any change. Defaults to `false`

security_groups.description: A list of security group IDs to assign to the LB

subnet_mapping.description: A list of subnet mapping blocks describing subnets to attach to load balancer

subnets.description: A list of subnet IDs to attach to the LB. Subnets cannot be updated for Load Balancers of type `network`. Changing this value for load balancers of type `network` will force a recreation of the resource

tags.description: A map of tags to add to all resources

target_groups.description: Map of target group configurations to create

timeouts.description: Create, update, and delete timeout configurations for the load balancer

vpc_id.description: Identifier of the VPC where the security group will be created

xff_header_processing_mode.description: Determines how the load balancer modifies the X-Forwarded-For header in the HTTP request before sending the request to the target. The possible values are `append`, `preserve`, and `remove`. Only valid for Load Balancers of type `application`. The default is `append`

launch_template_arn.description: The ARN of the launch template

launch_template_default_version.description: The default version of the launch template

launch_template_id.description: The ID of the launch template

launch_template_latest_version.description: The latest version of the launch template

launch_template_name.description: The name of the launch template

block_device_mappings.description: Specify volumes to attach to the instance besides the volumes specified by the AMI

cpu_options.description: The CPU options for the instance

default_kms_key_id.description: The ARN of the KMS key to use for EBS encryption

default_version.description: Default Version of the launch template

disable_api_stop.description: If true, enables EC2 instance stop protection

disable_api_termination.description: If true, enables EC2 instance termination protection

ebs_optimized.description: If true, the launched EC2 instance will be EBS-optimized

elastic_gpu_specifications.description: The elastic GPU to attach to the instance

hibernation_options.description: The hibernation options for the instance

iam_instance_profile_arn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an existing IAM instance profile.

image_id.description: The AMI from which to launch the instance

instance_initiated_shutdown_behavior.description: Shutdown behavior for the instance. Can be `stop` or `terminate`. (Default: `stop`)

instance_market_options.description: The market (purchasing) option for the instance

instance_type.description: The type of the instance. If present then `instance_requirements` cannot be present

key_name.description: The key name that should be used for the instance

launch_template_name.description: Name of launch template to be created

launch_template_tags.description: A map of additional tags to add to the launch template

launch_template_use_name_prefix.description: Determines whether to use `launch_template_name` as is or create a unique name beginning with the `launch_template_name` as the prefix

maintenance_options.description: The maintenance options for the instance

private_dns_name_options.description: The options for the instance hostname. The default values are inherited from the subnet

ram_disk_id.description: The ID of the ram disk

security_groups.description: A list of security group IDs to associate

tag_specifications.description: The tags to apply to the resources during launch

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to resources

update_default_version.description: Whether to update Default Version each update. Conflicts with `default_version`

user_data.description: The Base64-encoded user data to provide when launching the instance

cloudwatch_config_generated.description: The final CloudWatch Agent configuration (for debugging)

agent_properties.description:
Map of agent properties (static / metadata) keyed by agent name. Supported keys:
- dynatrace: object with:
    server_endpoint (string, required)
    network_zone   (string, required)
    host_group     (string, required)
    app_log_content_access      (bool, optional, default true)
    system_logs_access_enabled  (bool, optional, default false)
    monitoring_mode             (string, optional, default "fullstack")
    host_properties             (list(string), optional)
    host_tags                   (list(string), optional)

Example:
{
  dynatrace = {
    server_endpoint = "https://example.live.dynatrace.com:9999/communication"
    network_zone    = "az.aws.ase2"
    host_group      = "my-host-group"
    app_log_content_access     = true
    system_logs_access_enabled = false
    monitoring_mode            = "fullstack"
    host_properties            = ["infrastructure_managed=linux"]
    host_tags                  = ["app.short-name=dummy-app"]
  }
}

compliance_settings.description:
Settings controlling compliance / security agent supervision.
services: list of systemd service names.
retry_attempts: number of retries to start a service.
retry_delay: seconds to sleep between retries.
start_timeout: seconds to wait for active state.

egress_proxy_url.description: The egress proxy url in format of http://<host>:<port>.

no_proxy.description: Comma-separated list of domains/ips that should not use the proxy.

syslog_forwarding_host.description: Syslog forwarding host configuration on TLS.

user_data.description: The Base64-encoded user data to provide when launching the instance(cloud-config, shell, or multipart)

route53_record_fqdn.description: FQDN built using the zone domain and name

ignore_records_changes.description: Ignore terraform change on records' attribute.

records.description: List of objects of DNS records

arn.description: The ARN assigned by AWS to this instance profile

name.description: The name of the instance profile
The name of the instance profile

name_prefix.description: IAM instance profile name prefix. Use only if name is not specified.

path.description: The path of the instance profile in IAM

role.description: Name of the role to add to the profile

tags.description: A map of tags to add to all resources.

additional_policies_arn.description: List of IAM policy ARNs to attach to the role

additional_policies_json.description: Map of policies in JSON format to attach to the IAM role

assume_role.description: Assume role trust relationship in JSON format

inline_policy_json.description: IAM inline policy document in JSON format

max_session_duration.description: Maximum duration (in seconds) for IAM role session

tags.description: A map of tags to assign to resources.

use_policy_name_prefix.description: Determines whether names are used as a prefix

use_role_name_prefix.description: Determines whether names are used as a prefix

default_tags.description: Default tags for all supported resources

pcdb_dynamic_data.description: Dynamic Data fetched from data sources

pcdb_raw_data.description: Entire PCDB dataset for current application

pcdb_raw_data_filepath.description: Optional filepath of PCDB YAML filepath (defaults to platform_configuration_data.yml in the root module directory)

block_device_mappings.description: Specify volumes to attach to the instance besides the volumes specified by the AMI

client_keep_alive.description: Client keep alive value in seconds. The valid range is 60-604800 seconds. The default is 3600 seconds.

component_name.description: Name of the EC2 autoscaling component

create_lb.description: Indicates whether a LB is to be created

default_instance_warmup.description: The time, in seconds, that newly launched instances should be allowed to warm up before being included in the metrics.

desired_capacity.description: The desired capacity of the auto scaling group.

ebs_optimized.description: If true, the launched EC2 instance will be EBS-optimized

enable_cloudwatch.description: If true, the user's defined cloudwatch config will be injected.

external_lb.description: If true plus Internet Gateway exists, the LB will be public. Defaults to `false`

health_check_type.description: `EC2` or `ELB`. Controls how health checking is done

initial_lifecycle_hooks.description: One or more Lifecycle Hooks to attach to the Auto Scaling Group before instances are launched. The syntax is exactly the same as the separate `aws_autoscaling_lifecycle_hook` resource, without the `autoscaling_group_name` attribute. Please note that this will only work when creating a new Auto Scaling Group. For all other use-cases, please use `aws_autoscaling_lifecycle_hook` resource

instance_maintenance_policy.description: If this block is configured, add a instance maintenance policy to the specified Auto Scaling group

instance_refresh.description: If this block is configured, start an Instance Refresh when this Auto Scaling Group is updated

instance_type.description: The type of the instance. If present then `instance_requirements` cannot be present

launch_template_version.description: Launch template version. Can be version number, `$Latest`, or `$Default`

listeners.description: Map of listener configurations to create

load_balancer_type.description: The type of load balancer to create. Possible values are `application`, `gateway`, or `network`. The default value is `application`

log_collection_list.description: List of absolute log file paths to collect. Each entry is a string beginning with '/'.

max_size.description: The maximum size of the auto scaling group.

min_size.description: The minimum size of the auto scaling group.

network_zone_split.description:
Optional split of network zones between ASG instances and Load Balancer.
If provided, it overrides `network_zone`.
Required keys: `asg`, `lb`.
Allowed values:
  - asg: enterprise | interaction | transitional
  - lb : enterprise | interaction | transitional | internetIngress | agnIngress

Example:
network_zone_split = {
  lb  = "interaction"   # LB  will use interaction subnets
  asg = "enterprise"   # ASG will use enterprise subnets
}

retention_in_days.description: Retention in days applied uniformly to the single CloudWatch log group and all collected log streams.

scaling_policies.description: Map of target scaling policy schedule to create

source_ami_name.description: The name of the ec2 image to be inherited while baking.

target_groups.description: Map of target group configurations to create

use_app_dynatrace_host_group.description: If true the Dynatrace host_group will append <app_short_name> after <management_zone_id>-<account_id>.

user_data.description: The Base64-encoded user data to provide when launching the instance(cloud-config, shell, or multipart)

wait_for_capacity_timeout.description: The time to wait for the desired capacity to be achieved before timing out.
